summary: April 5, 2016, was marked by significant events in both the political and sports arenas. In the U.S., the Wisconsin presidential primaries were a key focus, with Senator Ted Cruz leading over Donald Trump among Republicans, and Bernie Sanders narrowly ahead of Hillary Clinton on the Democratic side. Meanwhile, in sports, Villanova University clinched the NCAA men's basketball championship in a dramatic finish against the University of North Carolina, thanks to a buzzer-beating three-pointer by Kris Jenkins. These stories captured the nation's attention, reflecting pivotal moments in the 2016 presidential race and college basketball history.

stories.headline: Wisconsin Primaries: Cruz Leads, Sanders Narrowly Ahead
stories.summary: The Wisconsin primaries were crucial in the 2016 U.S. presidential race. Senator Ted Cruz led Donald Trump by five points among Republicans, while Bernie Sanders had a slight edge over Hillary Clinton among Democrats. Despite Sanders' recent wins, Clinton maintained a delegate lead. The results were seen as pivotal for both parties' nominations.

stories.headline: Villanova Wins NCAA Title with Buzzer-Beater
stories.summary: Villanova University won the NCAA men's basketball championship in a dramatic game against the University of North Carolina. The game was tied in the final seconds after a three-pointer by Marcus Paige of North Carolina. Villanova's Kris Jenkins then scored a three-pointer at the buzzer, securing a 77-74 victory and the championship title.
